prompt: create a counter using 7 segment display. every time user hit button, the number increse, after 9 goes back to 0

trace.text:
Your button counter circuit is ready! 🎉 Here's a full breakdown:

---

## 🔌 Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Function |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| **D2** | 7-Seg segment **a** (via 220Ω) | Top segment |
| **D3** | 7-Seg segment **b** (via 220Ω) | Top-right segment |
| **D4** | 7-Seg segment **c** (via 220Ω) | Bottom-right segment |
| **D5** | 7-Seg segment **d** (via 220Ω) | Bottom segment |
| **D6** | 7-Seg segment **e** (via 220Ω) | Bottom-left segment |
| **D7** | 7-Seg segment **f** (via 220Ω) | Top-left segment |
| **D8** | 7-Seg segment **g** (via 220Ω) | Middle segment |
| **GND** | 7-Seg **common cathode** | Display ground |
| **D9** | Button pin **a** | Button input (INPUT_PULLUP) |
| **GND** | Button pin **b** | Button ground |

Each segment has its own **220Ω series resistor** to limit current safely.

---

## 📟 How It Works

| Action | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Press button | Count increments by 1 |
| Count reaches 9 → press again | Wraps back to **0** |
| Startup | Always shows **0** |

- Uses **INPUT_PULLUP** on D9 — button connects pin to GND when pressed (active LOW)
- **Falling-edge detection** prevents multiple counts per press
- **50ms debounce delay** avoids false triggers from mechanical bounce

---

## ⚡ Power Budget

| Item | Current Draw |
|------|-------------|
| Each lit segment | ~(5V − 2V) / 220Ω ≈ **13.6mA** |
| Max segments lit (digit 8) | 7 × 13.6mA ≈ **95mA** (shared across 7 pins) |
| Per Arduino pin (1 segment) | **~13.6mA** ✅ (well under 20mA limit) |
| Button pin | ~0mA (INPUT_PULLUP, no current at rest) |
| **Total draw** | **≤ 95mA** ✅ (Uno supports ~200mA total I/O) |

All pins are safely within the 20mA per-pin limit and the board's total budget. ✅
